Fake draft worksheet image source for traceability tests.

vocabulary.studentDefinition: Acceleration describes how velocity changes over time.
vocabulary.teacherDefinition: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.
vocabulary.misconception: Students may think acceleration only means speeding up.
vocabulary.exampleQuestion: A skateboarder speeds up each second. What is changing?
vocabulary.exampleAnswer: The skateboarder's velocity is changing, so the skateboarder is accelerating.
vocabulary.sourceTextSnippet: Acceleration is a change in velocity over time.

vocabulary.studentDefinition: Inertia is an object's tendency to keep doing what it is already doing.
vocabulary.teacherDefinition: Inertia is resistance to a change in motion and depends on mass.
vocabulary.misconception: Students may describe inertia as a force.
vocabulary.exampleQuestion: Why does a book stay still until something pushes it?
vocabulary.exampleAnswer: The book has inertia, so it stays at rest unless a force changes its motion.
vocabulary.sourceLocation: image region x=120 y=340 w=420 h=160

concepts.title: Acceleration as Velocity Change
concepts.studentExplanation: Acceleration happens when speed changes, direction changes, or both change.
keyIdeas: Acceleration can mean slowing down or changing direction.
examples: A bike speeding up after a stoplight is accelerating.
nonExamples: A cart moving at a steady speed in a straight line is not accelerating.
commonMisconceptions: Only faster motion counts as acceleration.
concepts.sourceTextSnippet: Acceleration can be any change in velocity.

concepts.title: Forces Can Change Motion
concepts.studentExplanation: A force can start motion, stop motion, speed something up, slow something down, or turn it.
keyIdeas: Unbalanced forces change an object's motion.
examples: A kick can start a soccer ball moving.
nonExamples: Balanced forces do not change motion.
commonMisconceptions: Every force always changes motion immediately.
concepts.sourceLocation: image region x=80 y=210 w=520 h=210
concepts.sourceTextSnippet: Forces can change how an object moves.

referenceFormulas.title: Acceleration

variables.meaning: the time interval during the change

referenceFormulas.studentExplanation: Acceleration compares the change in velocity with the time it took.
referenceFormulas.sourceTextSnippet: Acceleration equals change in velocity divided by time.

problemBank.question: A cart changes from slow to fast over 4 seconds. What idea describes that change?
problemBank.expectedAnswer: The cart is accelerating because its velocity changes over time.
problemBank.sourceTextSnippet: When velocity changes over time, acceleration is happening.

problemBank.question: A still ball starts rolling after a student pushes it. What changed the ball's motion?
problemBank.expectedAnswer: The push was a force that changed the ball's motion.
problemBank.sourceLocation: image region x=100 y=520 w=500 h=180
problemBank.sourceTextSnippet: A push can start an object moving.

standardsMap.description: Interpret simple representations of object motion.
Describe how balanced and unbalanced forces affect motion.

smokeTests.question: What does acceleration mean in this draft pack?
smokeTests.expectedAnswer: Acceleration means velocity is changing over time.
